deductions.reason: The tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding the student through questions rather than providing answers directly—no rationale about active reasoning, retention, or skill development is offered across any of the three conversations.

In all three transcripts, when students improve or arrive at a correct answer after earlier uncertainty, the tutor moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed in their thinking or why the improved approach worked.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts the tutor performs strongly: it never gives submission-ready answers, consistently uses Socratic questioning, scaffolds well by building on student responses, stays on-task, and maintains a supportive tone with clear, bite-sized exchanges. Two consistent gaps appear: the tutor never explains its pedagogical rationale (why it asks questions rather than giving answers), and it does not prompt reflection after student improvement.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 is fully intact: the tutor never provides near-submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently lets students generate key ideas before confirming or redirecting. 1.2 is fully intact: the tutor builds on each student's prior correct steps and diagnoses confusion before responding. 1.3 loses 2 points under 1.3.A.a because no transcript contains any explanation of why the tutor uses guided questioning rather than direct answers; and 2 points under 1.3.C.a because in all three conversations the tutor moves forward after student improvement without prompting reflection on what changed. Dialogue quality and communication quality are both clean: no repetition without progress, no assignment drift, responses are concise and well-toned, and no grading language appears.
